skill.name: customer-support
skill.description: Elite AI-powered customer support specialist mastering conversational AI, automated ticketing, sentiment analysis, and omnichannel support experiences. Integrates modern support tools, chatbot platforms, and CX optimization with 2024/2025 best practices. Use PROACTIVELY for comprehensive customer experience management.
skill.summary: Elite AI-powered customer support specialist mastering conversational AI, automated ticketing, senti...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported one external command pattern and multiple weak cryptographic algorithm patterns. Review found no executable code, shell command, cryptographic implementation, network access, or prompt injection attempt in SKILL.md, so the detections are false positives.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Resource Opening Instruction
low_findings.description: The static external command finding at line 27 is an instruction to open a local support resource when examples are needed. It is not Ruby backtick execution and does not invoke a shell command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line contains plain Markdown text and an inline file path reference. There is no command execution syntax or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts are caused by ordinary customer support terminology, such as AI platforms, decision trees, workflow design, and escalation examples. No cryptographic code or algorithm selection is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are descriptive support guidance, not code. No hashing, encryption, randomness, or security library usage appears in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Support teams need consistent responses, faster routing, and clear escalation paths. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design customer support workflows, chatbot flows, and knowledge resources.
actual_capabilities: Designs chatbot flows for common support questions and handoffs.
Creates ticket routing, prioritization, and escalation workflows.
Drafts support knowledge base outlines, FAQs, and troubleshooting guides.
Plans customer satisfaction measurement using CSAT, NPS, and CES.
Suggests omnichannel support practices for email, chat, social, and phone.
Builds support team training and quality assurance checklists.
limitations: Does not connect directly to helpdesk, CRM, or analytics systems.
Does not verify private customer data or live account history.
Requires human review before customer-facing policies are published.
Cannot replace legal, privacy, or compliance review for regulated industries.

use_cases.title: Create a Helpdesk Workflow
use_cases.description: Map intake, tagging, routing, escalation, and follow-up steps for a growing support queue.

use_cases.title: Plan a Chatbot Experience
use_cases.description: Draft intents, fallback behavior, human handoff rules, and sample responses for a support bot.

use_cases.title: Improve Self-Service Content
use_cases.description: Turn recurring support issues into searchable help center articles and troubleshooting guides.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a Support Goal
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for basic workflow help.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define a customer support workflow for [product]. The main issue is [issue], and the support channels are [channels].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Ticket Routing Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for structured operations planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a ticket routing and escalation workflow for [team size] agents handling [ticket types]. Include priority rules and handoff criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Chatbot Script
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for conversational support design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a chatbot flow for [customer scenario]. Include intents, clarifying questions, fallback responses, and human handoff triggers.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Support Metrics
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for measurement and process improvement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this support process: [process details]. Recommend improvements for CSAT, NPS, first response time, resolution time, and escalation quality.

output_examples.input: Create an escalation workflow for billing disputes.
output: A triage path for refund, invoice, and payment failure issues.
Priority rules based on account value, urgency, and financial impact.
Customer update templates for first response, investigation, and resolution.

output_examples.input: Design a chatbot for order status questions.
output: A conversation flow with order lookup, shipping status, delivery delays, and missing package paths.
Fallback prompts for unclear order details.
Human handoff rules for payment, fraud, or repeated delivery failures.

output_examples.input: Improve our help center for onboarding issues.
output: A topic map for account setup, activation, permissions, and first project creation.
Article outlines with clear titles and troubleshooting steps.
Metrics to track search success, ticket deflection, and article helpfulness.

best_practices: Provide product context, customer segment, support channels, and current pain points.
Ask for workflows that include ownership, escalation rules, and success metrics.
Review all generated customer-facing text for policy accuracy and brand voice.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to make final legal, privacy, refund, or compliance decisions.
Do not paste sensitive customer data unless it is required and allowed.
Do not publish chatbot responses without testing fallback and human handoff paths.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps design support workflows, chatbot flows, help center content, escalation paths, and customer experience measurement plans.

faq.question: Does it connect to Zendesk or Intercom?
faq.answer: No. It can suggest workflows for those platforms, but it does not perform live integrations.

faq.question: Can it write customer-facing replies?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft replies, but a team member should review them before sending.

faq.question: Can it analyze real support tickets?
faq.answer: It can analyze ticket text provided in the prompt. Remove unnecessary personal data before sharing examples.

faq.question: Is it useful for small teams?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create simple processes for shared inboxes, chat tools, and early help centers.

faq.question: What should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Share the product context, customer type, issue volume, support channels, policies, and target metrics.
